  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which scope should this review prioritize? A) Substantive/technical review focusing on contribution framing, logic, and evidence–claim linkage (R1.1–R1.4), B) Line-level copyediting and clarity/tone polish only (R1.6), or C) Both A and B.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Assess the high-level framing of the SG-Constraint contribution and the stated controller-design takeaway.",
    "R1.2": "Check the internal argument from benchmark design and results to the conclusion that tube-MPC dominates safe-RL baselines.",
    "R1.3": "Verify that the reported scores and violation-rate statements support the claims made about performance and task subsets.",
    "R1.4": "Evaluate the asserted novelty of the suite and the methodological claim relative to typical prior approaches (without a full literature audit).",
    "RX.1": "Flag where interpretations are uncertain given that only a single section is provided.",
    "RX.2": "Avoid endorsing generalizations that exceed what the presented evidence and scope can justify.",
    "RX.3": "Ensure the review remains confined to this section and the agreed responsibilities.",
    "RX.4": "Avoid recommendations that could mislead about safety-critical control performance or best practices.",
    "RX.5": "Tie each review point to specific sentences or claims in the provided section for traceability."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which scope should this review prioritize? A) Substantive/technical review focusing on contribution framing, logic, and evidence–claim linkage (R1.1–R1.4), B) Line-level copyediting and clarity/tone polish only (R1.6), or C) Both A and B.

rationale.R1.1: Assess the high-level framing of the SG-Constraint contribution and the stated controller-design takeaway.
rationale.R1.2: Check the internal argument from benchmark design and results to the conclusion that tube-MPC dominates safe-RL baselines.
rationale.R1.3: Verify that the reported scores and violation-rate statements support the claims made about performance and task subsets.
rationale.R1.4: Evaluate the asserted novelty of the suite and the methodological claim relative to typical prior approaches (without a full literature audit).
rationale.RX.1: Flag where interpretations are uncertain given that only a single section is provided.
rationale.RX.2: Avoid endorsing generalizations that exceed what the presented evidence and scope can justify.
rationale.RX.3: Ensure the review remains confined to this section and the agreed responsibilities.
rationale.RX.4: Avoid recommendations that could mislead about safety-critical control performance or best practices.
rationale.RX.5: Tie each review point to specific sentences or claims in the provided section for traceability.
